_comment_authorized_use_only: AUTHORIZED USE ONLY. This is a teaching artefact. Do NOT attach this policy to any real principal in a real account. The policy contains a deliberately broad permission grant for instructional purposes; read the SOLUTIONS.md walkthrough to understand what is wrong with it, why a scanner would flag it, and how to rewrite it correctly.
_comment_exercise: Exercise 1 — Policy Anatomy. Read this policy top-to-bottom. Identify, in writing in your notes: (1) the four required JSON keys present; (2) the optional keys present; (3) every action and every resource the policy grants; (4) every condition that gates the grant; (5) at least three things wrong with the policy from a least-privilege standpoint; (6) the specific Prowler check IDs (or Checkov check IDs) you would expect to fire against a principal carrying this policy. The SOLUTIONS.md walkthrough covers all six.
_comment_attach_target_hint: If you were to attach this hypothetically, the policy is structured as an IAM identity-based policy (no Principal field) intended for an IAM role used by a CI build pipeline. The role would assume a deployer identity to push container images to ECR and write build artefacts to S3. That is the stated purpose; whether the policy actually scopes to that purpose is what you are evaluating.
